pages.raw_text:
The special session of the coun-
Gil called last night by Mayor
Moore listened to committee re-
port on the bridge question which
was submitted by it's committee.
The committee believes the rail-
roads that are expected to stand
part of the expense of new struc-
lures will act fairly. No attack
was made upon any of the corpora
ton.


The only objection came from the
Washington Water Power Co., the
officials of which believed that the
Wallace-Coates plans, which the
council has already accepted, made
the arches of the concrete bridge
proposed for Howard st. too low for
high water. There were no repre-
sentatives present from the railway
companies.


The council adopted a motion
that Engineer Mcintyre draw new
plans for the proposed bridges and
that they be submitted to an en-
gineer of national reputation, for
bis approval. The Washington
Water Power Co. agreed to stand
half the expense of this engineers
services. Thus enters another w.
w. P. bridge expert, of whom there
have been many.


A committee consisting of Mayor
Moore, Councilmen N S. Pratt,
John Gray, Leonard Funk and w.
G. Estep was appointed to further
consult with railroad interests re-
garding the general pans of the
bridges and the proportion of ex-
pense to be borne. A report is ex-
pected in time for submission Tues
day night to the committee of the
whole meeting. ]. p. O'Brien. gen.
eral manager of the o. R. & N. is
expected to be present then and
consult with the council over the
Washington st. viaduct, which is
proposed. He favors a steel viaduct
instead of a concrete l., as stated by
the bridge committees report. The
ACCESSIBILITY of such plans will be
taken up with him.
